Post Comment
Traded up
QuoteReply
# Jun 30 2003 at 6:17 PM Rating: Decent
animekenji
1,123 posts
Traded up to this today from a Robe of the Keeper. Liked the sort of greyish-lavenderish color better than the red of the old robe. I payed a lot less than the average price listed here for this as I went to bazaar with only 100PP in my pocket and came away with about 6 INT/mana boosting items for my little gnome necro. Most of what I got was yard sale junk, but when you're playing it the hard way without being twinked, powerlevelled or bankrolled by a rich friend or alt anything is better than nothing.
____________________________
If you were me and I were you, would we still be friends?

Monk Item
QuoteReply
# Mar 06 2003 at 9:16 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591772224888
6 posts
I was given this item by a very nice twink troll in the Oasis, my kind hearted thanks goes out to you still. I have upgraded alot on my monkie, but not the CCS. For low weight, good ac, and str, it has served me quite well to my mid thirties.


Dish 35 Monk
Lasal 31 Pally
Mariache 28 Bard
▲ Page top
step up
QuoteReply
# Feb 19 2003 at 4:13 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593966319257
5 posts
▲ Page top
RE: step up
QuoteReply
# Feb 19 2003 at 4:16 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593966319257
5 posts
oops I wanted to know what i should upgrade to from ccs? I just reached 20th lvl so DING. and I am looking for upgrades to armour now what is a step up from ccs and when i get it anyone on antonious bayle intrested in getting a ccs?
▲ Page top
RE: step up
QuoteReply
# Apr 04 2003 at 2:30 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
Your CCS is the best piece of armor you have. upgrade everything else first.

Start by scrapping the serp bracers, for acrylia reinforced, or if money is a problem, bracers of erollis and hardened kodiak bracer.

Get gold/plat/or velium fire wedding rings to replace the bandit rings.

Those earrings you're wearing have no stats. sell them and get orc fang earrings.

Upgrade the ulaks. Personally, I like fighting batons, but Cane of the Tranquil is nice also.

Get spider fur for neck, back, and waist.

Then start saving for haste item. I like silver chitin handwraps for monks.

Once you have upgraded most of your other gear, THEN worry about the robe. Its pretty decent!

Edited, Fri Apr 4 13:56:14 2003
▲ Page top
RE: step up
QuoteReply
# Feb 19 2003 at 12:19 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
Depends. What class? Monk, cleric, wizard, etc...

Obviously, the upgrade paths would vary.

▲ Page top
Price
QuoteReply
# Aug 10 2002 at 1:15 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591778824420
292 posts
The price on these is dropping. This is probably the most common item in the bazaar. I bought one for 75pp.
▲ Page top
RE: Price
QuoteReply
# Aug 15 2002 at 12:14 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592250928198
View User Forum
Sage
208 posts
What i've found in the Bazaar is that for many items, there'll be a number of folks selling them at the so-called "going" price, and someone else pricing it "to move". In the case of the CCS, i got mine for 50pp, but every other one for sale in the Bazaar that day was going for 200 minimum.
▲ Page top
RE: Price
QuoteReply
# Feb 28 2003 at 1:00 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592256328860
11 posts
I agree. You can see wild price swings on items. Last night I bought this baby for my new high elf enchanter alt for 15pp. The seller had sent out a note, "All my stuff goes!" and so I sent him a tell offering 15pp for the CCS. He went for it.

I have taken to keeping a spreadsheet of the items I'm looking for, and each time I visit the Bazaar I just do a search and go down the list to see what the current prices are. I also keep a "historic low" price for each item so that I have some sense of what the range can be.

Bottom line is if you have patience, you can usually over the course of a few sessions find something for a decent price.

Hope this helps,


Edited, Fri Feb 28 13:56:28 2003

▲ Page top
dwarf?
QuoteReply
# Apr 03 2002 at 6:01 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591770900583
30 posts
why not dwarf usable?
▲ Page top
RE: dwarf?
QuoteReply
# Apr 03 2002 at 7:05 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592253816602
41 posts
Dwarves can't wear robes. Same with Wood Elves, Ogres, Trolls, Barbarians, Halflings, and Vah Shir. You will never see a robe usable by these races.
▲ Page top
RE: dwarf?
QuoteReply
# Apr 04 2002 at 7:03 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592248322158
282 posts
There are a few robes that dwarves can wear, but of course there is still no robe graphic for them.

RE: appearence?
QuoteReply
# Feb 28 2002 at 2:16 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1596385396259
Sage
64 posts
It is a powder blue robe, the shade of blue you often seen associated with baby boys. It has a semi-circular cut out below the knees that shows the wearer's feet and shins.

Allisia Autumnrain of The Archangel
Druid of Tunare in the 39th Season
Tholuxe Paells
▲ Page top
RE: appearence?
QuoteReply
# May 31 2002 at 1:26 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361221908
74 posts
With the Luclin graphics, the robes look different depending on what your race is.. So not everyone will have the circular cut out below the knees, nor will they have a diamond on it.. It is a light blue robe though.. without Luclin graphics turned on the robe has no decorations.

Actually after looking at the fashion pics above I would have to say it's a steel blue-gray color. Some of them look more gray than blue but that may be that persons color settings..

Edited, Fri May 31 14:19:15 2002

Better than Wu's Shirt?
QuoteReply
# Dec 30 2001 at 6:54 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592252092257
5 posts
I'm new at this whole Monk thing =o(
Whould this be better than the Wu's Fighting Shirt? I mean, the ac is only slightly higher and it has no agi. on it which is our most importatnt stat......right?

Some please give their 2cp worth, I'm kida confused. If the Wu's shirt is better this robe is going to my little cleric i started...she is naked =o)

Mitzy
17th season Lady Monk
▲ Page top
RE: Better than Wu's Shirt?
QuoteReply
# Jan 02 2002 at 11:03 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592758779201
2 posts
Greetings~

IMHO I think this is a much better choice for a starting monk that Wu's...aye the ac isnt that much better and it lacks the agi bonus....but the str bonus helps a lot at the lower levels, as do the 2 points of extra ac...the int does help you learn skills faster (I had double attack mastered within 4 levels of gaining the skill, and my 1hb is nothing to sneeze at either :) Basically if ye can get this or have one already, forget the Wus...the weight is not so good ofr monks, but the stats help ye a bunch from the get go...I had one all the way (thanks to a guildy) until recently (thanks to a very kind person) from lvl 1-20...and now I plan on passing it along to my ailing necro :) Gl and hope ye enjoy it...monks are a blast:)
▲ Page top
RE: Better than Wu's Shirt?
QuoteReply
# Aug 15 2002 at 12:05 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593362243244
54 posts
I agree totally - I have this too and have had it from lvl 9, my skills stay maxed or very close to it and it helps a lot with new skills. I do, however, have lightweight other items and can afford the weight (13 stones total). You may not have this choice yet.

8 posts
I love this robe. As a druid, I get begged for SoW quite often, and I just recently gained the ability to port, so this robe plus /roleplay lets me disguise myself quite well. One person thought I was a cleric, and another said I looked like a newbie mage. :) Awesome.

# Nov 11 2001 at 7:45 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591765525919
Scholar
748 posts
I got the opposite problem. With my skull helmet (executioner's hood) a robe, and a blunt weapon, I often get mistaken for a shaman and begged for SoW. Once someone even gave me clarity, hoping to get SoW in return! The ironic thing is, shamans can't even wear this robe.

Could someone tell people that not every iksar in a robe with a skull hat is a shaman? Could just as easily be a monk, or even a necromancer.

the information about tradskills and stats, given directly from verant can be found at eqtraders.com under

▲ Page top
RE: Nice to see that monks get a wis item
QuoteReply
# Aug 14 2001 at 6:10 AM Rating: Decent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Yes that is true. Wisdom helps skills like BlackSmithing or Tailoring and not combat skills.
▲ Page top
RE: RE: Nice to see that monks get a wis item
QuoteReply
# Jan 08 2003 at 2:08 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591765525919
Scholar
748 posts
WRONG, wis helps raise ALL skills (if it's higher than your int; if you're int is higher, int controls how fast you raise your skills); inclusing combat skills. Try playing a troll warrior and then a gnome warrior, you'll notice the gnome's skills going up a lot faster.
▲ Page top
RE: RE: Nice to see that monks get a wis item
QuoteReply
# Aug 28 2001 at 9:36 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591764418523
11 posts
Actually blacksmithing's stat for upping skills is str (better success) and tailoring is dex (better success). Wis and int play very small roles for those skills (as well as jewelcraft, but not many monks go for that trade, hehe). That aside, no monk or melee class with exceptions to hybrids should ever make a point of getting int/wis gear over a class that uses them for main stats as it will do nothing for them when they should be focusing on sta/str/agi/dex. How would you feel if the wiz got a 25 ac, 10 str/sta/dex tunic over you? :-)

If a monk does want int/wis gear for trade skills because they enjoy doing them, weight won't mean jack to them as it won't be used in combat unless they get jumped at the forge or something. So if you find a 20 weight 10int/wis piece of armor, shove it in the bank and pull it out when you go to practice your skills, otherwise focus on your main stats, hehe.
▲ Page top
RE: Nice to see that monks get a wis item
QuoteReply
# Feb 13 2002 at 12:09 PM Rating: Decent
Jophiel
View User Forum
Liberal Conspiracy
TILT
Wrong. ALL trade skills use the higher of INT/WIS to determine skill raises. However, for smithing it adds in STR (and uses the highest of the three) and for tailoring and fletching it uses the highest of INT/WIS/DEX. Successes are determined solely by skill vs. item difficulty.

Someone with W:80 I:190 S:90 has an identical chance to skill up in smithing as someone with W:80 I:90 S:190. Or someone with W:189 I:189 S:190 for that matter.
____________________________
Belkira wrote:
Wow. Regular ol' Joph fan club in here.
▲ Page top
RE: Nice to see that monks get a wis item
QuoteReply
# Jan 08 2003 at 2:03 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591765525919
Scholar
748 posts

got for free
QuoteReply
# Aug 14 2001 at 12:02 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591761090559
Scholar
22 posts
OK let me just say that this is a great robe. The bad thing about them is that they're lore ( My cleric got one for free in crystal caverns because they drop so much everyone has one already and can't loot another. That same night me and my monk partner killed the herbalist 5 times and it dropped 4 times, got the pack once. Anyways, we both already had one robe and couldn't loot another so my suggestion to newbies is if you can somehow get to Crystal caverns and sit in there for an hour or two you can probably get one for FREE. 3 people got free shrouds from us because we couldn't loot. I wish they weren't lore ( i'd be so rich. This is on terris thule by the way and these are very common.

Netherus Drunarth
Lvl 30 cleric
